text.title: Deterministic Test Data Pattern Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the Deterministic Test Data Pattern methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: All randomness goes through a single seeded RNG instance; the seed is declared in the fixtures module and printed in test output. `Math.random()`/`random.random()` calls inside test code are forbidden.
rule.rationale: un-seeded randomness is the most common flake source.

rule.statement: Time-dependent code MUST be wrapped in a clock abstraction; tests freeze the clock to a declared ISO-8601 instant. `new Date()` / `datetime.now()` in test paths are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Wall-clock leaks make tests fail on year/timezone boundaries.

rule.statement: Generated identifiers (uuid, ulid, nanoid) MUST use a seeded factory that produces the same sequence each run. uuid4() in tests is forbidden.
Tests pin LC_ALL/Intl locale + timezone explicitly. Implicit host locale is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Number/date formatting changes silently across hosts.

rule.statement: Factories return fresh objects per call; they MUST NOT mutate shared state. Reused-object antipattern is rejected.
rule.rationale: Test-order leakage is the silent flake source even after seeds are pinned.

rule.statement: On failure, the fixtures module MUST print seed + frozen-clock + locale so the failure can be reproduced locally with one command.

When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running Deterministic Test Data Pattern under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
